channel.title: The U-Curve Brain
channel.description: A macro-autonomous cognitive filter. Optimizing for agency, perspective, and resilience.

item.title: (Research) What Can We Gain by Losing Infinity?
item.description:
Source: Quanta Magazine

This article explores ultrafinitism, a philosophical stance in mathematics that rejects the concept of infinity. It examines why this view has been historically dismissed as heresy and details how it is now generating novel insights within mathematics and other fields, challenging conventional understanding.

item.title: (Research) “Only After Closed Doors”: Secrecy and Transparency in the Making of Educational Policy and Anthropological Research
item.description:
Source: Anthropology News

This article analyzes the dynamics of secrecy and transparency in educational policy formation and anthropological research. It draws on Marilyn Strathern's concept of the "tyranny of transparency," examining how neoliberal audit culture impacts academic practices and the visibility of knowledge production within British institutions.

item.title: (Audio) January Series 2026 | Day 17 | Let's Talk About Dopamine
item.description:
Source: The Sober Rebel



This podcast episode elucidates the neurochemical role of dopamine and its connection to addiction and early sobriety. It explores healthier methods for experiencing dopamine release, moving beyond the transient spikes associated with alcohol consumption, to support sustainable habit change.

item.title: (Audio) January Series 2026 | Day 20 | Self-Efficacy & Sobriety
item.description:
Source: The Sober Rebel



This podcast episode examines the concept of self-efficacy and its critical role in sustaining long-term sobriety and habit change. It explains how developing self-efficacy can support individuals when initial motivation diminishes, offering a psychological framework for enduring behavioral transformation.

item.title: (Video) Business‑enabling environment: How Egypt🇪🇬 offers incentives to the private sector for job creation
item.description:
Source: World Bank Group



This video details how Egypt is implementing incentives to foster a business-enabling environment, aiming to stimulate private sector growth and job creation. It highlights the importance of regulatory certainty and macroeconomic stability in attracting investment. This initiative can contribute to geopolitical stability by fostering economic resilience in a key North African nation.

item.title: (Video) Access Bristol: Assistive Technology Accelerator
item.description:
Source: University of Bristol



This video describes Access Bristol, an assistive technology accelerator launched in 2025. It details the program's purpose of advancing technologies for disabled creative users through collaborative efforts, providing insight into innovation aimed at improving accessibility and inclusion in creative fields.

item.title: (Video) CultureQuest - Powering New Visitor Experiences with Museums
item.description:
Source: University of Bristol



This presentation explores how Generative AI (GenAI) is being utilized to innovate visitor experiences within museums. It outlines methods for creating interactive engagements, such as simulated conversations with historical or mythological figures, demonstrating AI's potential to transform cultural education and accessibility.

item.title: (Video) When the Earth Had Supermountains
item.description:
Source: PBS Eons



Supermountains are so ridiculously enormous that, as far as we know, they've only ever formed twice, far back in deep time, and ...

item.title: (Video) Why do mammals keep evolving into ant-eaters?
item.description:
Source: PBS Eons



This video investigates the repeated evolutionary convergence of mammals into ant-eating forms. It explores the biological pressures and environmental factors that drive diverse mammalian species to develop similar specialized adaptations for consuming insects, providing insight into convergent evolution.

item.title: (Video) UCL Lunch Hour Lecture | Rebels, Refugees and Radical Ideas
item.description:
Source: UCL



This lecture details how University College London (UCL) revolutionized language learning between 1826 and 2026. It explores the influence of "rebels, refugees, and radical ideas" on pedagogical advancements, offering insights into historical and future shifts in language education.

item.title: (Video) Global military spending has almost doubled: Is the world a safer place?
item.description:
Source: Oxford Martin School



This video explores how global security is being reshaped by increased military spending, climate change, and artificial intelligence. It examines whether the near doubling of global military expenditure contributes to a safer world, providing insights into future security challenges and their drivers.

item.title: (Adage) \If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself, but to your estimate of it; and this you have the power to revoke at any moment.\ - Marcus Aurelius
item.description: A timeless principle for cognitive resilience.

item.title: (Reminder) Impression formation gives disproportionate weight to negative traits.
